violations.text: Frozen containers of food observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

Noodles in the kitchen, containers in the walk in cooler, and boxes of food in the basement observed stored on the floor. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: portable fan grill stove shelf above coffee/sauce area chest freezers have an accumulation of frost and debris wok unit has an accumulation of food debris and carbon build-up. New Violation. Correct By: 04/27/2026
2 lights beneath the hood were nonfunctional during inspection. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2026

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Food contact surfaces of the ice machine were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
Numerous food were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen, walk in cooler, and basement storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

A cleaver is stored on an unclean surface (side of the prep unit along wall) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
Water observed in blender. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
